title: Method for determining result of transmission for contention-based pusch in a wireless communication system and a device therefor
abstract: The present invention relates to a wireless communication system. More specifically, the present invention relates to a method and a device for determining result of transmission for contention-based PUSCH in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: transmitting a MAC PDU using a CB grant to an eNB; starting a timer when the UE transmits the MAC PDU using the CB grant; monitoring an ACK indication transmitted by the eNB while the timer is running; considering that the MAC PDU transmission using the CB grant is failed if the UE doesn't receive the ACK indication from the eNB until the timer expires; and retransmitting the MAC PDU to the eNB.
claims: Claims ( 16 ) A method for a User Equipment (UE) operating in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: transmitting a Medium Access Control (MAC) Protocol Data Unit (PDU) using a Contention-Based (CB) grant to an e-NodeB (eNB); starting a timer when the UE transmits the MAC PDU using the CB grant; monitoring an Acknowledgement (ACK) indication transmitted by the eNB while the timer is running; considering that the MAC PDU transmission using the CB grant is failed if the UE doesn't receive the ACK indication from the eNB until the timer expires; and performing a failure procedure corresponding that the MAC PDU transmission using the CB grant is failed. The method according to claim 1, wherein the UE keeps monitoring the ACK indication from the eNB in each subframe, while the timer is running. The method according to claim 1, wherein the ACK indication comprises one of reception of Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) masked by Contention Based-Radio Network Temporary Identifier (CB-RNTI) associated with the CB grant, or reception of PDCCH masked by Cell-RNTI (C-RNTI), or reception of PDCCH masked by C-RNTI and corresponding Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) includes UL grant for new transmission. The method according to claim 1, wherein the failure procedure comprises that the UE retransmits the MAC PDU using the CB grant for the eNB. The method according to claim 1, wherein the failure procedure comprises  that the UE initiates Buffer Status Reporting (BSR), Scheduling Request (SR), or Random Access (RA) procedure to obtain UL grant. The method according to claim 1, wherein when the ACK indication is received, the timer is stopped or reset. The method according to claim 1, wherein when the UE receives an ACK indication from the eNB, the UE discards the MAC PDU transmitted using the CB grant, and generates a new MAC PDU to be transmitted using CB grant. The method according to claim 1, wherein the timer is configured per the UE or per the CB grant. A User Equipment (UE) operating in a wireless communication system, the UE comprising: a Radio Frequency (RF) module; and a processor configured to: control the RF module, transmit a Medium Access Control (MAC) Protocol Data Unit (PDU) using a Contention-Based (CB) grant to an e-NodeB (eNB); start a timer when the UE transmits the MAC PDU using the CB grant; monitor an Acknowledgement (ACK) indication transmitted by the eNB while the timer is running; consider that the MAC PDU transmission using the CB grant is failed if the UE doesn't receive the ACK indication from the eNB until the timer expires; and perform a failure procedure corresponding that the MAC PDU transmission using the CB grant is failed. The UE according to claim 9, wherein the UE keeps monitoring the ACK indication from the eNB in each subframe, while the timer is running. The UE according to claim 9, wherein the ACK indication comprises one of reception of Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) masked by Contention Based-Radio Network Temporary Identifier (CB-RNTI) associated with the CB grant, or reception of PDCCH masked by Cell-RNTI (C-RNTI), or reception of PDCCH masked by C-RNTI and corresponding Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) includes UL grant for new transmission. The UE according to claim 9, wherein the failure procedure comprises that the UE retransmits the MAC PDU using the CB grant for the eNB. The UE according to claim 9, wherein the failure procedure comprises that the UE initiates Buffer Status Reporting (BSR), Scheduling Request (SR), or Random Access (RA) procedure to obtain UL grant instead of the retransmitting the MAC PDU. The UE according to claim 9, wherein when the ACK indication is received, the timer is stopped or reset. The UE according to claim 9, wherein when the UE receives an ACK indication from the eNB, the UE discards the MAC PDU transmitted using the CB grant, and generates a new MAC PDU to be transmitted using CB grant. The UE according to claim 9, wherein the time is configured per the UE or per the CB grant.
